info.name: 05-AdminEntryPoints
info.description: Do the dashboard admin refund levers work? Exercises /initiate (auto-approve), reject (creates nothing), modified-approve (the admin override component set drives the amount, not the customer's rf1), terminal-state re-respond guards, and list+pagination on one ride — with quick checks that money moved (the detailed ledger role-joins and invoice-field assertions are in 01).

exec: var envType = pm.environment.get('envType'); var msu = pm.environment.get('mockServerUrl') || ''; var url = pm.request.url.toString();
if (envType && envType !== 'Local' && msu && url.indexOf(msu) > -1) { if (pm.execution && typeof pm.execution.skipRequest === 'function') pm.execution.skipRequest(); }
// Refund amounts self-captured in THIS run's gate (rideId/order/T/cap), NOT carried from 00 — matches _amounts_note.
// rf1 = 0.5*cap (primary), rf2 = 0.25*cap (secondary partial), rf3 = 0.5*cap. Sized off the REAL RIDE_FARE
// component cap (rideFareCap, captured from fare_breakup_info) — NOT off T — so rf1 never spuriously trips
// the per-component cap on a ride with fees/toll/parking. Falls back to T until the gate sets rideFareCap.

item.name: Rider Auth
Rider OTP
Gate: latest COMPLETED booking for fixed rider (run 00 first)

if (b) { pm.environment.set('customer_bookingId', b.id); console.log('Gate: booking ' + b.id + ' status ' + b.status); }
var d = {}; try { d = pm.response.json(); } catch(e) {}
// the list handler can 500 transiently on an unrelated async notification fork — retry once before failing
pm.sendRequest({url: _gu, method: 'GET', header: {token: pm.environment.get('rider_token')}}, function (err, res) {
var d2 = {}; try { d2 = res.json(); } catch(e) {}

item.name: Capture BAP ride id (SQL — poll until drained)

exec: if(!rows.length){ pm.test('BAP ride row drained (run 00 first)', function(){ pm.expect(0,'no ride for booking; run 00').to.be.above(0); }); return; }
// NOTE: r.id is the RIDE id. orderId is a SEPARATE payment_order GUID, captured next via domain_entity_id.

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{ mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody() } }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } else { _finish([]); } }); }

item.name: Capture payment order short id + intent (SQL — poll until drained)

exec: if(!rows.length){ pm.test('payment_order row present', function(){ pm.expect(0,'no payment_order for ride').to.be.above(0); }); return; }

pm.test('payment_order row present (orderId != rideId)', function(){ pm.expect(o.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(o.id, 'orderId must be a real GUID, not the rideId').to.not.eql(pm.environment.get('rideId')); });
pm.test('order has a Stripe payment intent (pi_)', function(){ pm.expect(/^pi_/.test(String(o.payment_service_order_id))).to.eql(true); });

item.name: Capture RIDE_FARE component cap (SQL fare_breakup_info — refund cap source)

exec: // RIDE_FARE cap = sum of these tags (= li.rideFare + li.gstAmount, RidePayment.hs:841). TOLL/PARKING analogous (same pattern for tollCap/parkingCap).
function _sum(arr, tags){ return (arr||[]).filter(function(b){ return tags.indexOf(b.description) >= 0; }).reduce(function(a,b){ return a + Number(b.amount||0); }, 0); }
if (rows && rows.length) { try { bk = JSON.parse(rows[0].fare_breakups || '[]'); } catch(e) { bk = []; } }
console.log('Gate: rideFareCap='+rfCap+' tollCap='+tollCap+' parkingCap='+parkCap+' (from fare_breakup_info; refund amounts fall back to T if rideFareCap=0)');
pm.test('RIDE_FARE component cap captured from fare_breakup_info', function(){ pm.expect(rfCap, 'zero RIDE_FARE cap — stale fixture, re-run 00').to.be.above(0); });

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{ mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody() } }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } else { _finish([]); } }); }

item.name: Finalize refund env (riderToken alias + sanity gate)

exec: // 01-05 raise refunds as the customer via {{riderToken}}
pm.test('sanity: T captured and positive', function () { pm.expect(T).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test('sanity: all refund ids captured', function () {
console.log('Refund env ready. T =', T, pm.environment.get('txn_currency'), '| commission =', pm.environment.get('bpp_commission'));
// Refund amounts sized off the REAL RIDE_FARE component cap (rideFareCap from fare_breakup_info),
// falling back to T if the cap was not captured. rf1=0.5*cap, rf2=0.25*cap, rf3=0.5*cap.

item.name: Reset refund state for the ride (clean start)

exec: // SYNCHRONOUS single clear — the runner waits for THIS request, so no async re-fire can wipe a later-created refund_request.
pm.test('refund state reset (synchronous clean start)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Clear mock overrides (avoid leaked async state from an interrupted prior run)

exec: pm.test('mock overrides cleared (clean slate)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201,204]); });

item.name: Switch City for BAP (Rider Dashboard)

exec: pm.test('BAP dashboard switch OK', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Raise Refund Request rf1 = T/2 (Customer)

exec: pm.test('raise accepted', function () { if (pm.response.code === 400 && /refundable cap/i.test(pm.response.text())) { pm.expect(false, 'Raise rejected by the per-component cap: rf1 (T/2) exceeds the RIDE_FARE component total. Reduce the T-fraction. Body: ' + pm.response.text()).to.be.true; } pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Capture refundRequestId (SQL — after drain)

exec: pm.test('OPEN captured', function () { var d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.count, JSON.stringify(d)).to.be.above(0); pm.environment.set('refundRequestId', d.rows[0].id); pm.expect(d.rows[0].status).to.eql('OPEN'); });

item.name: Admin LIST shows the request (list API — coverage gap)

exec: pm.test('list returns the just-raised request', function () {

item.name: Reject the request (Admin respond approve=false)

exec: pm.expect(d.status, 'a rejected request must not be REFUNDED').to.not.eql('REFUNDED');
pm.expect(d.refundStatus, 'a reject returns refundStatus=Nothing -> JSON null/absent (NOT a real enum value)').to.be.oneOf([null, undefined]);

item.name: Assert request not REFUNDED + no refunds_id (SQL)

exec: try { var _rows = (_resp.rows) || []; console.log('  ↳ DB rows:', JSON.stringify(_rows.map(function(x){ return {st:x.status, amt:x.amount, total:x.total_amount, type:(x.reference_type||x.invoice_type||undefined)}; }))); } catch(e) {}
pm.test('request positively REJECTED + no refunds_id (reject moved no money)', function () {
pm.expect(r.status, 'the OPEN->REJECTED transition must be positively confirmed (not merely != REFUNDED)').to.eql('REJECTED');
pm.expect(r.refunds_id == null || r.refunds_id === '', 'no refunds_id on a rejected request').to.be.true;

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }

item.name: Assert reject initiated NO Stripe refund for THIS request (refunds_id NULL)

exec: pm.test('reject initiated no Stripe refund (this request refunds_id IS NULL)', function(){ pm.expect(rows.length).to.be.above(0); pm.expect(rows[0].status).to.eql('REJECTED'); pm.expect(rows[0].refunds_id==null || rows[0].refunds_id==='', 'a rejected request must not carry a refunds_id').to.be.true; });

item.name: Assert reject created NO BAP refund leg for THIS request (entity-ref scoped)

exec: // Scoped to THIS reject's refundRequestId via the leg's entity reference: the shared ride may carry OTHER refunds' legs.

// A 0-row result is the PASS condition here, so it is only meaningful if the query actually ran against a populated refundRequestId. Without these two guards a broken query (missing column -> 500) or an unset id would return 0 rows and pass for the wrong reason.

pm.test('reject-leg query actually ran (0 rows must mean no legs, not a failed query)', function(){ pm.expect(_j.error, 'mock SQL error: '+JSON.stringify(_j.error)).to.be.undefined; pm.expect(_j, 'response must carry rows').to.have.property('rows'); pm.expect(pm.environment.get('refundRequestId'), 'refundRequestId must be set, else the scope is empty').to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; });
try{ console.log('  \u21b3 BAP legs for THIS reject:', JSON.stringify(mine.map(function(x){return {st:x.status,type:x.reference_type};}))); }catch(e){}

pm.test('reject created NO BAP refund ledger leg for this request (entity-ref scoped)', function(){ pm.expect(mine.length, 'a rejected request must post no BAP refund leg stamped with its refundRequestId').to.eql(0); });

item.name: Assert reject created NO BPP refund leg for THIS request (entity-ref scoped)

exec: // Scoped to THIS reject's refundRequestId via the leg's entity reference: the shared ride may carry OTHER refunds' legs.

// A 0-row result is the PASS condition here, so it is only meaningful if the query actually ran against a populated refundRequestId. Without these two guards a broken query (missing column -> 500) or an unset id would return 0 rows and pass for the wrong reason.

pm.test('reject-leg query actually ran (0 rows must mean no legs, not a failed query)', function(){ pm.expect(_j.error, 'mock SQL error: '+JSON.stringify(_j.error)).to.be.undefined; pm.expect(_j, 'response must carry rows').to.have.property('rows'); pm.expect(pm.environment.get('refundRequestId'), 'refundRequestId must be set, else the scope is empty').to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; });
try{ console.log('  \u21b3 BPP legs for THIS reject:', JSON.stringify(mine.map(function(x){return {st:x.status,type:x.reference_type};}))); }catch(e){}

pm.test('reject created NO BPP refund ledger leg for this request (entity-ref scoped)', function(){ pm.expect(mine.length, 'a rejected request must post no BPP refund leg stamped with its refundRequestId').to.eql(0); });

item.name: Assert reject created NO Commission invoice for THIS request (reference_id-scoped)

exec: // the Commission refund invoice is keyed reference_id=refundRequestId, so it IS scopable to this reject.
try{ console.log('  \u21b3 invoices for THIS refundRequestId:', JSON.stringify(rows.map(function(x){return {type:x.invoice_type,st:x.status};}))); }catch(e){}

pm.test('reject created NO Commission refund invoice for this request', function(){ var comm=rows.filter(function(r){return r.invoice_type==='Commission';}); pm.expect(comm.length, 'a rejected request must not create a Commission refund invoice').to.eql(0); });

item.name: Admin Initiate Refund (auto-approve)

exec: pm.expect(false, 'Initiate rejected by the per-component cap (rf2 exceeds the remaining RIDE_FARE cap). Body: ' + pm.response.text()).to.be.true;

item.name: Assert Admin-Initiated Row Born Approved (SQL)

exec: function _ok(rows){ return rows.some(function(r){ return r.code==='ADMIN_INITIATED' && (r.status==='APPROVED' || r.status==='REFUNDED'); }); }
try { console.log('  ↳ ADMIN_INITIATED rows:', JSON.stringify(admin.map(function(x){ return {st:x.status, code:x.code, tries:x.refunds_tries}; }))); } catch(e) {}
pm.test('Initiate creates exactly one ADMIN_INITIATED request', function () {
pm.expect(admin.length, 'expected exactly one ADMIN_INITIATED refund_request born of /initiate').to.eql(1);
pm.test('ADMIN_INITIATED row born APPROVED/REFUNDED (auto-approve, never OPEN)', function () {

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }

item.name: Quick Check BAP Refund Ledger Settled (SQL)

exec: // Quick check only: the BAP role-join + per-component sum are asserted in 01. Here just confirm money moved -> >=1 SETTLED refund-typed leg.
try { console.log('  ↳ SETTLED refund legs:', JSON.stringify(settled.map(function(x){ return {st:x.status, type:x.reference_type}; }))); } catch(e) {}
pm.test('Initiate moved money: >=1 SETTLED refund leg (full model asserted in 01)', function () {
pm.expect(settled.length, 'expected >=1 SETTLED refund raise leg (zero-out reversals excluded) on the ride after /initiate auto-approve').to.be.above(0);

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }

item.name: Raise Refund Request rf1 (Customer, for modified-approve)

exec: pm.test('raise accepted', function () { if (pm.response.code === 400 && /refundable cap/i.test(pm.response.text())) { pm.expect(false, 'Raise rejected by the per-component cap: rf1 exceeds the remaining RIDE_FARE cap after the initiate refund. Body: ' + pm.response.text()).to.be.true; } pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Capture refundRequestId (modified — after drain)

exec: function _finish(rows){ if(!rows.length){ pm.test('Modified OPEN request captured', function(){ pm.expect(0,'no fresh OPEN request drained for the modified-approve raise').to.be.above(0); }); return; } rows.sort(function(a,b){ return String(b.created_at).localeCompare(String(a.created_at)); }); pm.environment.set('refundRequestId', rows[0].id); pm.test('Modified OPEN request captured (fresh, not the rejected one)', function(){ pm.expect(rows[0].id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(rows[0].status).to.eql('OPEN'); }); console.log('Modified refundRequestId='+rows[0].id); }

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select',method:'POST',header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},body:{mode:'raw',raw:_sqlBody()}}, function(e,r){ var rows=[]; try{rows=((r&&r.json())||{}).rows||[];}catch(x){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ setTimeout(function(){_poll(n-1);},3000); } else { _finish([]); } }); }

item.name: Modified Approve (admin override to rf2)
Assert Modified Approve Override Drove the Amount to rf2 (via linked refunds)

exec: console.log('  \u21b3 modified request:', JSON.stringify(rr.map(function(x){return {st:x.status, refunds_id:x.refunds_id};})), '| rf1='+_rf1+' rf2='+_rf2);
pm.test('Modified request reached REFUNDED', function(){ pm.expect(rr.length).to.be.above(0); pm.expect(rr[0].status).to.eql('REFUNDED'); });
pm.test('approved_refunded_components records the admin override (exactly one RIDE_FARE component ~= rf2)', function(){
var arc = rr.length ? rr[0].approved_refunded_components : null; if (typeof arc === 'string') { try { arc = JSON.parse(arc); } catch(e){ arc = null; } }
pm.expect(arc, 'approved_refunded_components must parse to an array').to.be.an('array');
pm.expect(Number(arc[0].amount), 'approved component amount ~= admin override rf2').to.be.closeTo(_rf2, 0.05);
if (rid && refs.filter(function(x){return x.id===rid;}).length===0 && k>0){ setTimeout(function(){_refPoll(k-1);},3000); return; } // KV->PG drain: wait for the linked refunds row

pm.test('the modified request issued a Stripe refund (admin lever completed end-to-end)', function(){ pm.expect(mine.length, 'the modified refund_request.refunds_id should link a refunds row').to.be.above(0); });

pm.test('admin override drove the refund amount to rf2 (not the customer rf1)', function(){ pm.expect(amt, 'a refund amount must be present for the modified request').to.not.be.null; pm.expect(Math.abs(amt-_rf2), 'refund amount must equal the admin override rf2='+_rf2+' (got '+amt+', customer rf1='+_rf1+')').to.be.at.most(0.05); });

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select',method:'POST',header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},body:{mode:'raw',raw:_rrBody()}}, function(e,r){ var rows=[]; try{rows=((r&&r.json())||{}).rows||[];}catch(x){} if(_ok(rows)||n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){_poll(n-1);},3000); } }); }

item.name: Terminal Re-Approve Errors (already REFUNDED)

exec: pm.test('Re-approving a REFUNDED request is rejected (non-2xx)', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.code, 'a terminal REFUNDED request must not be re-approved/re-processed').to.not.be.oneOf([200,201]);
pm.test('Re-approve error says the request is already terminal', function () {

item.name: Terminal Re-Reject Errors (already REFUNDED)

exec: pm.test('Rejecting a REFUNDED request is rejected (non-2xx)', function () {
pm.expect(pm.response.code, 'a terminal REFUNDED request must not be rejected after the fact').to.not.be.oneOf([200,201]);
pm.test('Re-reject error says the request is already terminal', function () {
